title: Aporum longipecten
taxonomy: Aporum longipecten is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified within the genus Aporum. Aporum longipecten is a type of epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants for support. The classification of Aporum longipecten is further divided into two subspecies: Aporum longipecten var. longipecten and Aporum longipecten var. gracile. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and current botanical research.

intro: Aporum longipecten, also known as the Long-pectinated Dendrobium or simply Long-pectinated Orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. Bioactive constituents of Aporum longipecten include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aporum longipecten is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tonics and other medicinal preparations.
